title: Cosmos Mic Tunnel — mic-reactive perspective tunnel whose speed and twist ride loudness and ring radii ride the spectrum
summary: An audio-reactive tunnel: particles and glowing rings fly down a perspective bore toward a pulsing core. The fly speed and the swirl twist ride the overall loudness, while each ring's radius (and a per-segment warp wobble) rides the spectrum, so the bore pulses, accelerates, and warps to the beat. It reacts to the MICROPHONE live (gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable/denied) and runs a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Pure Canvas 2D, self-contained, zero dependencies.
usage_notes: RUN: open & click "enable mic" to react to your voice/music; falls back to a built-in beat if no mic. KEY TECHNIQUES: an AnalyserNode (fftSize 128) feeds a 64-bin spectrum + RMS level into render(spectrum,level,t); tunnel fly speed = 0.10+level, twist rate = 0.25+level so loudness drives motion; rings recede in z with focal-length perspective and their radius = spectrum bin value, plus a per-segment wobble sampled from neighbouring bins for warp; particles stream down the bore with depth-scaled size/alpha; central core glow pulses with level; synthSpectrum (mulberry32-seeded sum of moving formant bumps + a 4-kick-per-loop beat pulse) drives auto/fallback deterministically and loops seamlessly. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: NP (particle count), NR (ring count), speed/twist gains, FL focal length, ring radius range, core pulse depth.
